core_mechanism: stochastic regularization by independent Bernoulli deletion of a fixed fraction of a system's components on each iteration of an iterative fitting process, which prevents components from co-adapting and implicitly averages the fit over an exponentially large ensemble of randomly-thinned subsystems
brief_reason: The abstract describes randomly omitting half of the feature detectors on each training case, which prevents complex co-adaptations and forces each unit to be useful across combinatorially many internal contexts — i.e. random per-iteration component dropout acting as an ensemble-averaging regularizer.
